title: sambucus mexicana subsp. cerulea
intro: Sambucus mexicana subsp. cerulea, also known as blue elderberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its blue-violet flowers and small, black berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of blue elderberry include its potential to boost the immune system, reduce the severity of flu symptoms, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular remedy for respiratory issues and infections. Blue elderberry can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
